info.name: Auth – Password Reset (3-step: forgot-password → verify-reset-code → reset-password)
info.description:
E2E tests for the 3-step password reset flow.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-password):
  1. setup             — register gmail_test_email, fetch verification OTP from Gmail, verify email
  2. happy-path        — login (capture pre-reset token) → forgot-password → Gmail OTP →
                         verify-reset-code wrong code → 422, verify-reset-code correct code → 200 (fpw_reset_token),
                         reset-password (fpw_reset_token) → 200, login new pw → 200, login old pw → 401,
                         /me with pre-reset token → 401 (blocklist), verify-reset-code replay → 422,
                         reset-password with stale grant token → 422
  3. anti-enumeration  — unknown email → 202, unverified email → 202
  4. validation        — all 422/413 input-error cases for forgot-password, verify-reset-code, reset-password
  5. rate-limiting-fpw — shared fpw:ip: bucket (burst=3): 3 warmups → 4th → 429
  6. rate-limiting-rpw — shared rpw:ip: bucket (burst=5): 5 warmups → 6th → 429

All test users use @e2e.test emails (or gmail_test_email) so `make _e2e-db-clean` can wipe them.
Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before this collection.

Happy-path sequence note:
  POST /verify-reset-code validates the OTP WITHOUT consuming it and returns a short-lived
  reset grant token (UUID, 10 min TTL in KV store). POST /reset-password then takes the
  grant token + new_password and atomically consumes the OTP and updates the password hash.
  The grant token is single-use: it is deleted immediately after the first POST /reset-password read.

Notes on what is NOT covered here and why:
  - Cooldown suppression: HTTP surface is always 202; covered by service_test.go.
  - Locked/inactive account anti-enumeration: requires DB seed; covered by service_test.go.
  - Mail delivery failure → 202: handler_test.go TestHandler_ForgotPassword_AlwaysReturns202.
  - Timing invariant: unit-level assertions on authshared.GetDummyOTPHash.
  - Same-password rejection: grant token is consumed on first reset-password call, so a 2nd
    call needs a new grant token; covered by handler_test.go and service_test.go.
  - Blocklist failure → 200: handler_test.go TestHandler_ResetPassword.
  - Max-attempts lockout → 429: requires a 2nd Gmail OTP fetch; covered by service/handler tests.
  - Expired OTP → 410: requires waiting full OTP TTL; covered by service_test.go.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// The rate-limiting folders override _xff to 127.0.0.1 via their own prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, and verifies the email. All subsequent folders depend on fpw_email being verified in the DB.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in fpw_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] fpw_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full 3-step password-reset scenario in deterministic order:
  1.  Login → capture pre-reset access token (fpw_pre_reset_token)
  2.  POST /forgot-password → 202, fetch password-reset OTP from Gmail (fpw_otp)
  3.  POST /verify-reset-code wrong code (000000) → 422 (ErrInvalidCode, attempt counter = 1)
  4.  POST /verify-reset-code correct fpw_otp → 200 (capture fpw_reset_token, grant TTL = 10 min)
  5.  POST /reset-password (fpw_reset_token + fpw_new_password) → 200 (OTP consumed, grant deleted)
  6.  POST /login with fpw_new_password → 200 (confirms password updated in DB)
  7.  POST /login with fpw_password (old) → 401 (confirms old password rejected)
  8.  GET /me with fpw_pre_reset_token → 401 (confirms blocklist via KV store)
  9.  POST /verify-reset-code with fpw_otp (now consumed) → 422 (ErrTokenNotFound)
  10. POST /reset-password with stale fpw_reset_token → 422 (grant key deleted from KV)

exec: // Stash this token BEFORE the reset so we can later verify it is blocklisted.
console.log('[INFO] Pre-reset access token saved as fpw_pre_reset_token.');
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and verified, a password reset code has been sent');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for password-reset email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API for password-reset OTP...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching password-reset email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode password-reset email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in password-reset email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Password-reset OTP stored in fpw_otp:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] fpw_otp is empty — Gmail fetch in forgot-password step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
// Submit a deliberately wrong 6-digit code (000000). This is well below the
// max-attempts threshold (5) so the token remains active for the next step.
// ErrInvalidCode is mapped to validation_error by the handler.
// The attempt counter in DB is now 1. No grant token is issued.
// Correct OTP: service validates without consuming the token.
// Response body contains a short-lived reset grant token (UUID, 10 min TTL in KV).
// Capture it as fpw_reset_token for use in the next step.
console.log('[INFO] Grant token saved as fpw_reset_token:', body.reset_token);
// Grant token + new password: handler reads the grant from KV (then deletes it),
// calls ConsumeAndUpdatePasswordTx which consumes the OTP, updates the password
// hash, revokes all active sessions, and writes an audit row.
// The grant token is single-use and is now gone from the KV store.

item.name: POST /login with NEW password → 200 (confirms password hash updated in DB)

exec: // A 200 confirms the new password hash is persisted in the DB.

item.name: POST /login with OLD password → 401 (confirms old password rejected after reset)

exec: // The old password must no longer work after the reset.

item.name: GET /me with PRE-RESET access token → 401 (blocklist active via KV store)

exec: // The handler writes a per-user block key ('pr_blocked_user:<uid>') to the KV
// store after a successful reset. The auth middleware checks this key and rejects
// all access tokens for the user until the TTL expires (= access token lifetime).
// This confirms the KV store integration is wired end-to-end.
// fpw_otp was consumed by the successful reset above.
// Attempting to verify it again must return 422 validation_error (ErrTokenNotFound).

item.name: POST /reset-password → 422 (stale grant token — deleted after first use)

exec: // fpw_reset_token was deleted from KV immediately after the successful reset.
// Re-submitting it must return 422 validation_error ('invalid or expired reset token').

item.name: anti-enumeration
item.description:
Verifies the anti-enumeration contract for POST /forgot-password:
  every path — unknown email, unverified account — must return 202 with the identical body.
  No hint about whether an OTP was issued is ever revealed.

exec: // Ghost email: no user row exists. Service short-circuits at GetUserForPasswordReset
// (ErrUserNotFound), calls GetDummyOTPHash for timing equalisation, returns zero result.
// Handler always responds with the same 202 body.
pm.test('body is the correct anti-enumeration message', () => {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and verified, a password reset code has been sent');

item.name: [forgot-password] email exists but account is unverified → 202 (anti-enumeration)

exec: // Register a fresh @e2e.test account inline but do NOT verify it.
// Service short-circuits at the !user.EmailVerified guard, returns zero result.
// The HTTP response must be identical to the happy path (202 + same body).
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ display_name: 'FpwUnvfy', email: email, password: 'P@ssw0rd1!' }) }
console.warn('[WARN] Could not register fpw-unvfy account:', err || res.code);
pm.test('body is the correct anti-enumeration message', () => {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and verified, a password reset code has been sent');

item.name: validation
item.description:
All input-validation cases for both POST /forgot-password and POST /reset-password.
Validation fires at the handler level before any DB or service call,
so non-existent emails and any 6-digit code are safe to use throughout.
The 413 cases use a valid JSON body with a large _pad field so MaxBytesReader fires
during JSON decode (matching the behaviour of /request-unlock and /login).

exec: // 246 'a's + '@e2e.test' (9 bytes) = 255 bytes — one over the 254-byte RFC limit.
// Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit using a valid JSON field.
// http.MaxBytesReader fires during json.Decoder.Decode → respond.DecodeJSON maps to 413.
pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: 'vpc-bigbody@e2e.test', code: '123456', _pad: pad }) });
// validateResetPasswordRequest calls authshared.ValidatePassword before the service.
// An empty password returns ErrPasswordEmpty (which IsPasswordStrengthError == true).

item.name: [reset-password] new_password too short (< 8 chars) → 422 (representative strength case)

exec: // validateResetPasswordRequest validates password strength before the service is called.
// ErrPasswordTooShort is mapped to validation_error by the handler.
// This one case confirms the strength-check path is wired end-to-end.
// All other rule variants (no-upper, no-lower, no-digit, no-symbol, >72 bytes)
// are covered by handler_test.go and service_test.go.

item.name: rate-limiting-fpw
item.description:
Verifies the fpw:ip: IP rate limiter for POST /forgot-password (burst=3, rate=3/10 min).

The folder prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests share one bucket.

Sequence:
  1. warmup 1/3 — POST /forgot-password (slot 1) → not 429
  2. warmup 2/3 — POST /forgot-password (slot 2) → not 429
  3. warmup 3/3 — POST /forgot-password (slot 3) → not 429
  4. 4th request — POST /forgot-password → 429 (bucket exhausted)

Covers checklist item: 4th request from same IP within 10 min → 429 (fpw:ip:, burst=3).

exec: // Pin all requests in this folder to 127.0.0.1 so they share one fpw:ip: bucket.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (fpw:ip: slot 3 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

item.name: rate-limiting-rpw
item.description:
Verifies the rpw:ip: IP rate limiter for POST /reset-password (burst=5, rate=5/10 min).

The folder prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests share one bucket.
Warmup requests use an empty reset_token so they get 422 at handler-level validation,
but the rate-limit middleware fires first and each one still consumes a slot.

Sequence:
  1–5. warmup 1/5 … 5/5 → not 429 (slots 1–5 consumed)
  6.   6th request → 429 (bucket exhausted)

Covers checklist item: 6th request from same IP within 10 min → 429 (rpw:ip:, burst=5).

item.name: warmup 5/5 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (rpw:ip: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by rate-limiting folder prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email in setup/register prerequest. Used as the verified account for the happy-path and happy-path subfolder tests.
Initial password for fpw_email. Set in setup/register prerequest.
New password used in the successful reset step. Set in setup/register prerequest.
Verification OTP fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script. Consumed by setup/verify-email.
Password-reset OTP fetched from Gmail by happy-path/forgot-password test script. Reused for wrong-code, same-password, correct-reset, and replay steps in the happy-path folder.
Access token saved from the pre-reset login. Used in happy-path/GET /me step to verify the blocklist is active after the reset.
